content.full_text:
Public Affairs Support Services
401 Wythe Street, Suite 2-A, Alexandria, Virginia 22314  (703) 684-2915

February 25, 1992


Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, 6th Floor
Sacramento, California 95807

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     Public Affairs Support Services (PASS) provides administra-
tive assistance to United Parcel Service (UPS) in connection with
the operations of its separate segregated fund, the United Parcel
Service Political Action Committee (UPSPAC), FEC identification
No. C-00064766.

     The UPSPAC is organized pursuant to the Federal Election
Campaign Act.  It was formed and exists primarily for the purpose
of supporting political campaigns at the Federal level of govern-
ment and is registered with, and operates pursuant to, regula-
tions promulgated and administered by the Federal Election
Commission (FEC).  Accordingly, UPSPAC contributions are made
from funds received from stockholders, executive and administra-
tive personnel of UPS and their families.

     In calendar 1991, the UPSPAC made contributions to Califor-
nia non-Federal candidates.  As the UPSPAC had neither (1)
received any contributions in calendar 1991 which were earmarked
for making contributions to or expenditures on behalf of Califor-
nia State and local candidates, ballot measures or committees,
nor (2) made any such contributions or expenditures during the
previous calendar year, the UPSPAC did not consider itself to
have qualified as a "recipient committee" pursuant to California
law.  However, as the UPSPAC's contributions to California non-
Federal candidates exceeded $10,000 in calendar 1991, the UPSPAC
considered itself to have qualified as a "major donor committee"
and filed the appropriate reports with the State of California as
well as the county of Los Angeles and the city and county of San
Francisco.

     UPS has now determined to establish a non-Federal political
action committee for the purpose of supporting California State
and local candidates, ballot measures and/or committees.

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
February 25, 1992
Page Two

Following discussions with staff of the Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division, we understand that the following operating
and reporting procedures would be in compliance with California
law and regulations.

1.     The UPS will authorize the establishment of a separate
       political action committee, the United Parcel Service
       Political Action Committee -- California (UPSPAC-California). While the Chairman and Treasurer of the
       UPSPAC-California will be the same individuals as the
       Chairman and Treasurer of the UPSPAC, the UPSPAC-California will maintain its own bank account, separate
       and apart from the UPSPAC Federal account. This
       depository account will not necessarily be maintained
       in a banking organization authorized to do business or
       located in California. Nor will the Treasurer or
       Chairman of the UPSPAC-California necessarily be
       residents or electors of California.

2.     The UPSPAC-California will be the only political
       action committee maintained by UPS that will make contributions to California non-Federal campaigns, and
       all such contributions will only be made from the
       UPSPAC-California's sole depository account.

3.     The UPSPAC-California will only be funded by receiving
       an allocated portion of certain individual contributions to the UPSPAC's separate segregated fund. As
       you know, these UPSPAC monies, by Federal law, cannot
       include any corporate funds, and are received as the
       result of solicitations of UPS stockholders, executive
       and administrative personnel. Such solicitations make
       clear that contributions to the UPSPAC are used to
       support State and local as well as Federal campaigns,
       but do not specify any set allocation percentages.

4.     The receipts of the UPSPAC that are allocated to the
       UPSPAC-California will be provided in lump-sum amounts
       from the UPSPAC's depository account. These amounts
       will be based on a pre-determined percentage which may
       vary from year to year. The percentage allocation
       will apply to monies either (1) previously received
       during a defined period of time, or (2) to be received
       after a date certain and continuing until a pre-determined total amount has been deposited in the UPSPAC-California's depository account. During the period of
       time to which any such allocation is applied, all receipts of the UPSPAC will be subjected to such allocation.

Public Affairs Support Services

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
February 25, 1992
Page Three

     5.    The UPSPAC-California will maintain records of the
receipt of these allocated amounts as monetary contri-
butions from those contributors to the UPSPAC whose
contributions were received by the UPSPAC during the
UPSPAC-California allocation period(s). These amounts
will not be recorded or reported as receipts from
other political committees.

     6.    The amount that will be recorded as being contributed
to the UPSPAC-California by each such contributor will
be that amount based on the pre-determined allocation
percentage of each contributor's total contribution to
the UPSPAC during the period of time in which any
given UPSPAC-California allocation was in effect. If
any such allocated amount, either by itself or when
aggregated with any other allocation(s) attributable
to the same individual and made during the same calen-
dar year, equals $100 or more, then the date the
contribution was received by the UPSPAC-California, as
well as the full name, street address, city and State
of the individual contributor, his/her occupation and
name of employer, amount received during the period of
time covered by the pertinent California report, and
appropriate cumulative-to-date amount received from
the contributor will be itemized.

     7.    The UPSPAC-California's costs of administration and
solicitation will be paid by UPS. For the purposes of
compliance with the reporting of such non-monetary
contributions received by the UPSPAC-California, UPS
proposes to consider as non-monetary receipts those
actual administrative costs attributable solely to the
operations of the UPSPAC-California. That is, any
administrative costs -- including rent, office expens-
es, management fees, and all other overhead costs of
operating a political committee -- which are attribut-
able in whole or in part to the operations of the
UPSPAC or the UPS governmental relations activities
will not be included. Only those administrative costs
that would not otherwise be incurred but for the
operations of the UPSPAC-California, such as those
related to (1) the processing of receipts of the
UPSPAC-California; (2) the making of UPSPAC-California
contributions to California campaigns; (3) other
general maintenance of the UPSPAC-California bank
account; and (4) the preparation of campaign disclo-
sure filings and other reports required by the Cali-
fornia Political Reform Act, will be treated as non
-monetary contributions to the UPSPAC-California.


                                        Public Affairs Support Services

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
February 25, 1992
Page Four

As for solicitation expenses, UPS proposes to attrib-
ute a percentage of certain total UPSPAC solicitation
costs as non-monetary contributions by UPS to the
UPSPAC-California as follows: Let "A" equal the
dollar total of contributions received by the UPSPAC
between two consecutive solicitation efforts, any
portion of which is allocated to the UPSPAC-California. "B" equals that dollar portion of
total ("A") that is allocated to the UPSPAC-California. The percentage of "A" that "B" represents, or
"X," will be applied to "C," the total costs of the
earlier of the two solicitation efforts, and the
resulting amount will be the total dollar amount of
solicitation expenses that, when capable of being
determined, will be reported as a UPS non-monetary
contribution to the UPSPAC-California.

For example, suppose that a solicitation mailing is
sent out on January 1st of each year, at a cost to UPS
of $10,000.00 ("C"). In that particular calendar
year, $500,000 is ultimately raised ("A"). UPS allocates 10% of all monthly receipts beginning with
January of that same calendar year to the UPSPAC-California until $40,000.00 is raised ("B"). As
$40,000.00 represents 8% of $500,000.00 ("X"), UPS
would consider itself to have provided the UPSPAC-California with $800.00 (X% of C, or 8% of $10,000.00)
in non-monetary contributions in connection with the
solicitation of funds for the UPSPAC-California.

Finally, as to the reporting of such non-monetary
contributions, we understand that any UPS employee
time spent providing services to the UPSPAC-California
that is less than 10 percent of their total time in
any one month is not considered to be reportable as a
contribution from UPS. As to other expenses incurred
by UPS on behalf of the UPSPAC-California, such as
payment by UPS to PASS for any administrative services
provided in connection with the operations of the
UPSPAC-California, we understand that these are reportable.

Furthermore, we understand that any such payments, or
other similar payments made by UPS to other contractors in connection with administrative services or
solicitation expenses allocated to the UPSPAC-California, that exceed $100 in the aggregate within a calendar year must be itemized on the appropriate Schedule
showing non-monetary contributions received by the
UPSPAC-California.

Public Affairs Support Services

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
February 25, 1992
Page Five

     Finally, we have also been advised by Technical Assis-
tance and Analysis staff that while the "contributor"
should be reported as UPS, memo entries showing the
name and address of all recipients of payments by UPS
in excess of $100 for the provision of such services
as noted above and attributed to the UPSPAC-California
must also be provided on these same schedules.

8.   The UPSPAC will not consider its activities in connec-
tion with the UPSPAC-California to qualify the UPSPAC
as a California recipient committee nor a major donor
committee, and the UPSPAC will neither register nor
file as such.

     The UPSPAC-California wants to begin operations as soon as
possible. Therefore, the UPSPAC-California intends to file a
"Statement of Organization" as a State General Purpose Recipient
Committee with the California Secretary of State, the County of
Los Angeles and the City and County of San Francisco as soon as
it receives or spends $1,000 or more in connection with Cali-
fornia non-Federal elections. If there are any misunderstandings
as to the manner in which the UPSPAC-California intends to
operate, please advise me at once. I will be happy to see that
any questions are resolved or procedures adjusted to comply with
California law and regulations.

     Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Peter Leigh Snell
                                        Vice President

cc: Mr. Kenneth L. Schellie, UPSPAC Treasurer
    Mr. Tom Butler, UPSPAC Counsel


                                        Public Affairs Support Services
